# OpenClaw Memory Stack — Total recall, 90% fewer tokens

> Free and open source. MIT licensed. No subscription, no cloud API costs.
> GitHub: https://github.com/Apptah/openclaw-memory-stack

## What it does

OpenClaw's native memory loads full text every time, returns duplicate results, and lets old context pile up. Memory Stack fixes all three — and adds capabilities the native system doesn't have.

## How Memory Stack works

- **90% fewer tokens per search**: 3-tier output — L0 (~100t summary), L1 (~800t detail), L2 (full text). Only fetches what's needed.
- **5-engine parallel search**: BM25 full-text, vector/QMD semantic, DAG-compressed history, structured fact store, markdown files — all run in parallel, results merged with Reciprocal Rank Fusion.
- **Structured fact memory**: Extracts 8 fact types automatically — decisions, deadlines, requirements, entities, preferences, workflows, relationships, corrections. Negation-aware. Auto-supersede when facts change.
- **Smart dedup**: 3-level write-time dedup (exact, normalized, structured key). Never pay twice for the same memory.
- **Cross-agent sharing**: CLI (`openclaw-memory query/add/recent`), drop zone (`~/.openclaw/memory/external/`), unified global memory across workspaces.
- **Self-healing**: Auto-init, graceful fallback to keyword-only, 24h maintenance cycle.

## Technical details

- Runs 100% locally. All search and storage on-device. No data leaves your machine.
- No API keys required for core functionality. Optional: LLM for fact extraction (local Ollama/MLX or user-configured cloud endpoint).
- Installs as a native OpenClaw memory provider plugin
- Requires OpenClaw 2026.3.2 or later
- macOS + Linux. Windows via WSL2.

## Install

```bash
npx clawhub@latest package install openclaw-memory-stack
```

Or clone and run directly:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Apptah/openclaw-memory-stack
cd openclaw-memory-stack
./install.sh
```

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/Apptah/openclaw-memory-stack
- Issues / support: https://github.com/Apptah/openclaw-memory-stack/issues
